title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Beneficiary
The beneficiary is the person or legal entity to whom a draft is payable.
Branch Number
A branch number is a 5-digit number that identifies a branch of a Canadian financial institution. The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
FX Drafts
FX Drafts is the application used to quote rates, enter foreign currency drafts (including US dollars), and print drafts in-branch.
Institution Number
The institution number is a 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that identifies a financial institution (e.g., 809 for BC credit unions and 828 for Ontario credit unions). The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number. The institution number is sometimes known as the route number.
PCMLTFA
The
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act
(Canada).
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens.
User Management Security Officer
User Management Security Officers use the User Management application to add, modify, and delete Client Centre Users and to modify and delete other User Management Security Officers. User Management Security Officers are also referred to as Security Officers. User Management Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications.
END
